Title: Biosynthesis and functions of the plant sulfolipid

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Anionic thylakoid membrane lipid important for photosynthesis and adaptation to phosphate limitation; potential sulfur storage lipid
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Association with SQD1 is proposed to facilitate sulfite delivery and support SQDG biosynthesis; glu1 knockout mutants show decreased leaf SQDG content by about 20–25 mol% of total lipids compared with wild type
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Spinach SQD1 was purified as a 250-kDa complex containing FdGOGAT. 2. FdGOGAT co-purifies and tightly associates with SQD1 in the chloroplast stroma. 3. Because FdGOGAT can reversibly bind sulfite, it is proposed to channel sulfite to SQD1 and thereby support SQDG biosynthesis, although the exact in vivo role remains to be carefully examined.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Protein purification/co-purification; complex analysis; subcellular colocalization; tentative structural modeling; mutant phenotype observation
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Chloroplast stroma; also reported dual targeting to mitochondria and chloroplasts for FdGOGAT1
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Possible moonlighting functions complicate interpretation; exact contribution to SQDG pathway remains uncertain
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential auxiliary target to enhance SQDG synthesis by improving sulfite delivery, but use requires caution because its central role in nitrogen/photorespiratory metabolism may cause pleiotropic effects
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not classified as a pathway enzyme for SQDG itself in the review; rather a possible interacting/supporting factor. In Arabidopsis, glu1 knockout data are noted as unpublished and the role in SQDG biosynthesis remains tentative.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Anionic membrane lipid supporting photosynthesis and phosphate-starvation acclimation
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Altered auxin signaling via gain-of-function IAA14 suppresses phosphate starvation-induced SQDG accumulation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Pi starvation-induced membrane lipid remodeling genes are coactivated. 2. In slr-1, a gain-of-function mutant of IAA14, induction of genes for galactolipid and SQDG synthesis and phospholipid degradation is substantially reduced. 3. This leads to suppressed accumulation of SQDG under phosphate deficiency.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis under phosphate starvation; lipid profiling; gene expression analysis
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Mutants showed altered Pi homeostasis with high free Pi even under Pi deficiency
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Auxin signaling manipulation could alter membrane lipid remodeling under phosphate limitation, but broad developmental and Pi-homeostasis effects are expected
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because it is explicitly summarized as part of SQDG biosynthetic regulation in this review.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Anionic membrane lipid supporting photosynthesis and phosphate-starvation acclimation
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of ARF7 together with ARF19 suppresses phosphate starvation-induced SQDG accumulation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Auxin transport/signaling is important for membrane lipid remodeling under Pi starvation. 2. In arf7 arf19 loss-of-function mutants, Pi starvation-induced expression of genes including those for SQDG synthesis is substantially reduced. 3. These mutants show suppressed accumulation of SQDG under phosphate deficiency.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis under phosphate starvation; lipid profiling; gene expression analysis
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Altered Pi homeostasis and broad auxin-related developmental effects likely
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to tune phosphate starvation lipid remodeling, but pleiotropic auxin effects limit direct deployment
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Summarized jointly with ARF19 in the review as regulators of Pi starvation responses affecting SQDG accumulation.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Anionic membrane lipid supporting photosynthesis and phosphate-starvation acclimation
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of ARF19 together with ARF7 suppresses phosphate starvation-induced SQDG accumulation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Auxin signaling mediated by ARF7/ARF19 contributes to Pi starvation responses. 2. In arf7 arf19 mutants, induction of genes involved in SQDG synthesis is reduced. 3. SQDG accumulation is suppressed under Pi-deficient conditions.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis under phosphate starvation; lipid profiling; gene expression analysis
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Altered Pi homeostasis and broad auxin-related developmental effects likely
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to tune phosphate starvation lipid remodeling, but pleiotropic auxin effects limit direct deployment
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Summarized jointly with ARF7 in the review as regulators of Pi starvation responses affecting SQDG accumulation.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Sugar nucleotide-modifying enzyme; dimeric NAD+-binding enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Formation of the sulfoquinovose head-group donor
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts UDP-glucose and sulfite into UDP-sulfoquinovose, the activated sulfoquinovose donor for SQDG synthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate head-group donor required for target product synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Indispensable key biosynthetic enzyme; rate-limiting status not established
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Anionic membrane lipid important for photosynthesis, phosphate-starvation acclimation, and potentially sulfur storage
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for SQDG biosynthesis by producing UDP-sulfoquinovose
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SQD1 is the plant/algal ortholog of bacterial sqdB and is conserved in all SQDG-producing organisms. 2. Recombinant Arabidopsis SQD1 was biochemically shown to synthesize UDP-sulfoquinovose from UDP-glucose and sulfite. 3. SQD1 is a soluble chloroplast stroma enzyme and forms a complex with FdGOGAT in spinach, suggesting possible sulfite channeling in vivo.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genetic ortholog identification; recombinant enzyme biochemical assay; X-ray crystallography; subcellular localization; complex purification
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Coexpressed with UGP3 and SQD2 in Arabidopsis
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Phosphate depletion discussed as condition elevating pathway demand
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome coexpression context mentioned for pathway genes; no accession provided
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Dependence on sulfite supply and possible partner proteins/cofactors; exact in vivo sulfur donor mechanism not fully resolved
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Core engineering target for enhancing SQDG production; likely should be stacked with UGP3 and SQD2 and possibly consider sulfur-delivery factors
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The reaction rate of recombinant enzyme was slower than expected, suggesting possible requirement for cofactors/protein partners or alternative in vivo sulfur donor.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers sulfoquinovose to diacylglycerol to produce sulfoquinovosyldiacylglycerol
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Indispensable terminal determinant of SQDG formation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Anionic membrane lipid important for photosynthesis, phosphate-starvation acclimation, and potentially sulfur storage
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of SQD2 causes complete loss of SQDG in Arabidopsis; mutant growth is retarded under Pi-deficient conditions
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Plant SQD2 was identified as the ortholog of cyanobacterial sqdX. 2. Arabidopsis sqd2 knockout mutants completely lack SQDG. 3. Recombinant SQD2, together with SQD1, produces SQDG biochemically, confirming SQD2 as the plant SQDG synthase.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Ortholog identification; knockout mutant analysis; recombinant protein coexpression and biochemical assay; subcellular localization
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Coexpressed with UGP3 and SQD1 in Arabidopsis
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome coexpression context mentioned; no accession provided
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: sqd2 mutants show growth retardation under Pi-deficient conditions; PG remains high suggesting compensatory lipid remodeling
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Prime engineering target to increase or restore SQDG production, particularly for phosphate-limitation tolerance, likely in combination with UGP3 and SQD1
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The review also notes that SQDG and PG are not fully interchangeable; double reduction of anionic lipids strongly impairs growth/photosynthesis.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts glucose-1-phosphate to UDP-glucose in plastid stroma, supplying the substrate for SQD1
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for target product synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key precursor-supply enzyme for plastid SQDG synthesis; rate-limiting status not established
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Anionic membrane lipid important for photosynthesis, phosphate-starvation acclimation, and potentially sulfur storage
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: ugp3 knockout mutants lack SQDG under both normal and phosphate-depleted conditions
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGP3 was identified by transcriptome coexpression analysis as a gene highly coexpressed with SQD1 and SQD2. 2. UGP3 encodes a plastid UDP-glucose pyrophosphorylase that synthesizes UDP-glucose in the stroma. 3. Arabidopsis ugp3 knockout mutants lack SQDG under both normal and Pi-depleted conditions, indicating specific importance for SQDG biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcriptome coexpression analysis/database mining; knockout mutant analysis; metabolite/lipid profiling; subcellular functional assignment
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly coexpressed with SQD1 and SQD2
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly coexpressed with SQD1 and SQD2
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome coexpression analysis using ATTEDII and RIKEN PRIMe; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Glucose-1-phosphate is also substrate for ADP-glucose pyrophosphorylase in starch synthesis, suggesting possible flux competition between starch and SQDG biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful precursor-supply target for engineering SQDG production; flux balance with starch synthesis should be considered
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No major differences in other glycosides or cellulose-related compounds were reported between wild type and ugp3 mutants, suggesting specificity toward SQDG biosynthesis in plastids.
